@cbchang6200 Yes, I know. My recommendation wasn’t based on Grahams background, but rather on the themes talked about. Especially the complex emotions around parental abandonment. Many people who grow up in foster (myself being a foster kid)  struggle with those same feelings, so I felt it could be meaningful.
​@tanicatonnie7944Yes, your input is very meaningful. I thought that you were relating your comment to Graham.

@tanicatonnie7944- I appreciate your comment, having grown up in the foster care system myself. At the ages of 5&6 my brother and I were finally adopted into a family of already adopted children. As we all got older, 3 out of 5 of us wanted to look for our biological parents. While I did have a small desire to find out where my brother and I came from, and even started the process of looking, I stopped. I began to think ... what if I'm inviting chaos into my life? It could go either way. I didn't trust the process and eventually let it go. 2 of us found their parents and/or family members, but I just went on with my life.
